case_brief: Robert Alvarez, a 52-year-old warehouse foreman at Cascade Distributors, was terminated after requesting FMLA leave to care for his mother diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Alvarez submitted the FMLA paperwork on March 3. He was called into HR on March 8 and told his position was being eliminated due to 'operational efficiency improvements.' No other warehouse foreman positions were eliminated. A temporary worker hired to cover his leave period was later converted to a permanent employee in the same role. Alvarez had worked at Cascade for 19 years with no disciplinary record. The company employs 340 people and is covered by FMLA. Alvarez is employed at-will in Oregon (9th Circuit). Three weeks before submitting FMLA paperwork, Alvarez received a merit raise of 4%, suggesting positive standing. The 'operational efficiency' changes announced at his termination were never implemented as described in the company memo given to Alvarez.
